title: Fork Member Artifact Schema v0.1.1
description: Schema for individual workflow event artifacts within a Fork evidence packet. Each member must declare its artifact class, fixture identity, and authority attribution.

_fork_artifact_class.description: The declared class of this artifact. Governs its role in the packet and the authority distinctions Fork preserves for it.

_fork_fixture_id.description: Unique identifier for the fixture to which this artifact belongs.

_fork_fixture_class.description: Whether this artifact is part of a synthetic reference corpus or a production workflow event.

_fork_source_truth.description: Fork's position on the truth of the content. NOT_CLAIMED means Fork does not adopt or certify the content as true.

_fork_production_data.description: Declares whether production data is present. Must be NONE for synthetic corpus artifacts.

_fork_authority_note.description: Required plain-language statement of the authority boundary for this artifact type. Must distinguish what Fork records from what Fork establishes.

_fork_interpretation_authority.description: Identifies who holds the authority to interpret or resolve questions about this artifact that Fork does not address.

_schema_note: Domain-specific member properties remain extensible. Required evidentiary-frame fields are schema-declared. Class-specific semantic non-promotion rules are mechanically enforced by verify_public_disclosure.py.
